identitySentence: The Peugh Law Firm is a Denton criminal defense practice led by Daniel K Peugh, a former chief prosecutor in the Denton County District Attorney's office who has represented clients in DWI, drug, juvenile, and assault cases since 1998.

concept.headline: Charged in Denton County? Talk to a former prosecutor before you talk to anyone else.
concept.subheadline: An arrest or investigation is overwhelming, and the first days matter. The Peugh Law Firm offers a free consultation so you can understand where you stand and what comes next.
credibilityPoints: Former Chief Juvenile Prosecutor and Chief Misdemeanor Prosecutor in the Denton County Criminal DA's Office
Office at 721 W Mulberry Street in Denton

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: The Peugh Law Firm defends people facing criminal charges across Denton County, including DWI and DUI, drug possession and marijuana charges, juvenile defense, assault and family violence, theft, fraud and forgery, and probation violations. The firm also helps eligible clients clear their records through expunctions.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: Daniel K. Peugh served as a prosecutor in the Denton County Criminal DA's Office before entering private practice, and the firm works in Denton County courts from its office on West Mulberry Street in Denton.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your consultation starts with the firm listening: what you were charged with, when it happened, and what court dates are on the calendar. You will get a straightforward assessment of your situation and the options in front of you.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Dear Mr. Peugh and The Peugh Law Firm team,

Your firm has deep roots in Denton County's criminal courts: Texas bar admission in 1998, service as Chief Juvenile Prosecutor and Chief Misdemeanor Prosecutor in the Denton County Criminal DA's Office, and a practice today on West Mulberry Street handling DWI, drug possession, juvenile defense, assault and family violence, theft and fraud, probation violations, and record clearing.

A practice with that courtroom background deserves an intake process that keeps pace with it. Your website already does a solid job presenting your experience and offering a free consultation. What it cannot do today is finish the booking: there is no online scheduler, so every inquiry still waits on a phone call or a manual reply before a time is set. People facing criminal charges often reach out in the evening or on a weekend, and those messages can sit until the office opens.

The Lead Capturer closes that gap. It is a single, focused intake experience built for your firm alone:

- A custom identity line written for you: "The Peugh Law Firm is a Denton criminal defense practice led by Daniel K Peugh, a former chief prosecutor in the Denton County District Attorney's office who has represented clients in DWI, drug, juvenile, and assault cases since 1998."
- A custom contact form built around your practice areas, so the questions fit a DWI arrest, a juvenile matter, or an expunction inquiry rather than a generic template.

Here is what happens once someone reaches out:

1. The inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox. You see every message as it arrives.
2. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to their message. A DWI inquiry gets a DWI-relevant reply, not a form letter.
3. That response presents your available consultation times.
4. A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the time is held for someone committed to showing up.

We would rather prove this than promise it. We are offering a 15-day proof period: we build the complete experience for The Peugh Law Firm, and you test it end to end. If it does not earn its place in your practice, you walk away.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject identifies the firm and likely matter type.
6. Confirm Reply-To contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without staff action.
9. Confirm the customer receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails. Contact us and we will make it right before anything goes live.

Respectfully,
The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
